title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music, but isn’t banning it outright
slides: Tidal won't pay royalties for AI music—so why allow it?
Streaming platforms fear losing human artists to machines.
Tidal now accepts AI tracks but labels them clearly.
Labels must decide if AI music dilutes their catalog value.
Check your streaming revenue: is AI competing with you?
thread_text:
1. Why allow AI music?

Tidal won't pay royalties for AI tracks — yet they let artists upload them anyway.

2. Platforms need humans.

Streaming services fear losing top creators to machines that never ask for a raise.

3. Labels face a choice.

Every AI track accepted risks diluting the value of your catalog — or is it a new revenue stream?

4. Transparency is the fix.

Tidal now labels AI tracks clearly, but the label decides what that label is worth.

5. Check your streaming revenue.

Open your distributor dashboard and sort by AI-generated tracks. Are they eating your plays?
video_script: Tidal hosts AI music but won't pay royalties.
Streaming platforms fear losing human artists.
Tidal labels AI tracks for transparency now.
Labels decide if AI dilutes catalog value.
Check your streaming revenue for AI competition.

script: Tidal hosts AI music but won't pay royalties.
Streaming platforms fear losing human artists.
Tidal labels AI tracks for transparency now.
Labels decide if AI dilutes catalog value.
Check your streaming revenue for AI competition.

instagram.caption:
Tidal is now accepting AI-generated music on its platform, but will not pay royalties for those tracks. 🎵

The streaming giant requires AI tracks to be clearly labeled, yet allows them to compete directly with human artists for listeners. Platforms like this fear losing creators to machines that never demand payment.

If you're an artist or label, every AI song on your distributor's dashboard is now a potential competitor for your own streaming revenue. 🧾

Open your Spotify or Tidal for Artists page right now: how many of your recent monthly plays are being "won" by AI-generated tracks you never approved? 🤔

Open your Spotify or Tidal for Artists page right now: how many of your recent monthly plays are being "won" by AI-generated tracks you never approved? 🤔
